common.placeholderNotice: Company information (legal name, CEO, address, business registration number) is shown as [PLACEHOLDER] before the official launch. It will be filled in by a follow-up PR before the effective date.
common.noticeBanner: This document is a working draft of the standard SaaS terms and policies, augmented with ClassAuto's student-data protection and guardrail policies. Legal review will be performed separately before the effective date.
common.changeHistoryEmpty: This is the initial version — no prior change history.

meta.title: Terms of Service — ClassAuto
meta.description: Terms of Service for ClassAuto. Service scope, member rights and duties, billing/refund/cancellation, and student data protection — fourteen articles in total.

hero.title: Terms of Service
hero.subtitle: Standard terms governing use of the ClassAuto service. Billing, refund, cancellation and content rights mirror the company's published policy documents one-for-one.

blocks.text: These Terms ("Terms") govern the use of the ClassAuto service ("Service") provided by [PLACEHOLDER] ClassAuto Inc. ("Company"). They define the terms and procedures of use, the rights and duties of the Company and members, and other necessary matters.
The Company operates the Service primarily for academic adoption. The Terms combine standard SaaS provisions with ClassAuto-specific student-data protection and four-tier cost-guardrail policies.
The following terms are used throughout this document with the meanings below.

items: "Service" means the online educational SaaS suite provided by the Company under the name ClassAuto, including lecture-video generation, learning sessions, assessment, and analytics.
"Member" collectively refers to anyone who has accepted these Terms and uses the Service — including instructors, learners, and institutional administrators.
"Instructor" means a Member who uploads lecture material and operates lectures.
"Learner" means a Member who watches lecture videos and takes assessments shared by an instructor.
"Institution" means a school, department, college, or comparable body to which multiple Instructors and Learners belong.
"Content" means slide decks and other lecture material uploaded by a Member, together with derived scripts, videos, assessment items, and Q&A answers.
"AI Output" means any output generated by external AI providers (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI Embeddings, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Google Cloud, DeepL, etc.) used by the Company.
"Guardrail" means the four-tier policy (input limits, RAG semantic filtering, frequency caps, anomaly detection) the Company applies for cost and abuse control.

effect.title: Effect and amendment of these Terms

blocks.text: These Terms take effect when a Member registers for or starts using the Service. The Company may amend these Terms within applicable law.

When the Terms are amended, the Company will give at least thirty (30) days' notice in advance through the Service or by email before the amended Terms take effect. Amendments that are unfavourable to Members will be subject to an additional explicit consent step in addition to the notice.

Members who do not agree with an amendment may terminate their use of the Service before the effective date. Continued use of the Service after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the amended Terms.
The Company provides the following capabilities to Members. Detailed specifications follow the pricing policy and feature matrix published by the Company.

items: PPT upload → text extraction → AI script drafting → TTS synthesis → HeyGen avatar video pipeline
Assessment system that automatically authors multiple-choice and short-answer items (formative and summative)
Six-stage learning-session state machine and watch-progress tracking
Heartbeat-based attention monitoring with automatic pause on no-response
RAG Q&A backed by pgvector embeddings, restricted to lecture material
Stripe-based subscription billing (Free / Basic / Pro plans) with usage caps
Multilingual script translation via DeepL and Google Cloud Translate
Korean and English user interface
Instructor dashboard with attendance, accuracy, engagement and cost metrics, plus CSV export

blocks.text: The Company may add, modify, or discontinue features for quality reasons after prior notice. During the beta phase, certain features may be marked Experimental until stability is confirmed.
Registration is completed through the Company-provided form via institutional email or OAuth (Google), and by accepting these Terms and the Privacy Policy.

items: Instructor registration is verified using an institutional email (typically .ac.kr / .edu) or an alternative employment proof accepted by the Company.
Learner registration uses an institutional email or a learning code issued by an Instructor.
Members must keep their registered information accurate and up to date and update changes through the in-Service settings.
Members are responsible for safeguarding their credentials (password, auth tokens) and may not assign, lend, or share them with third parties.
Where another person's identity or institutional email is misappropriated, or where false information is provided, the Company may suspend or delete the account after prior notice.

blocks.text: Members must comply with these Terms, applicable law, the Company's usage policies, and the Guardrail policies. The following actions are prohibited.

items: Uploading or distributing material that infringes third-party copyrights, portrait rights, or personal information rights.
Interfering with the normal operation of the Service or impairing other Members' use.
Using automated tools (bots, macros, scrapers) to consume Service resources abnormally.
Using multiple accounts or sessions to bypass learner-side limits such as the single-stream concurrent playback cap.
Disguising off-topic questions as lecture material to circumvent the RAG semantic filter (similarity threshold 0.7).
Submitting or citing AI Output in a manner that violates the Member's institutional academic-integrity or research-ethics rules.
Violating applicable law or public order and morals.

blocks.text: Where a violation is identified, the Company may suspend the affected account in whole or in part after prior notice (or with subsequent notice in urgent cases). Automatic blocks last twenty-four (24) hours and are subject to review through the learner-protection process.
Intellectual property in Content uploaded to the Service belongs to the Member or the rightful owner. The Company may process such Content solely for Service purposes within the following scope.

items: Processing, copying, and transformation needed to perform Service functions — AI script generation, video rendering, automatic assessment item generation, etc.
Temporary storage required to host lecture videos and deliver them safely to Learners.
Production of anonymised aggregate data for operations, statistics, and quality improvement.
Retention required by law or administrative order.

blocks.text: The Company makes no warranty as to the accuracy, completeness, or fitness of AI Output. Members are responsible for reviewing AI Output before sharing it with Learners. Where institutional rules require disclosure or citation of AI Output, Members must comply on their own.
The Company will not use Member Content as advertising material, marketing material, or training data for external models without the Member's explicit consent.
Learning data (watch logs, Q&A questions and answers, assessment responses, attendance) is processed under the principles below. See the Privacy Policy and the Trust page (/trust) for full details.

items: Not used for advertising or marketing.
Per-learner data is visible only to the Instructor of the relevant lecture; department- or college-level views are limited to authorised institutional administrators.
Automatic deletion processes run when a Learner graduates or terminates the account (see Privacy Policy §5).
Learners may exercise the rights of access, rectification, erasure, portability, and restriction over their own data.

blocks.text: Minors under 14 may use the Service only after a guardian's consent has been obtained; the Company operates a separate minor-protection process.

The Service is offered in three plans — Free, Basic, and Pro. Pricing and features for each plan follow the policy published on the pricing page (/pricing). This Article mirrors the company pricing policy (01-pricing-policy.md §4) one-for-one.

items: Paid plans are sold as monthly or annual subscriptions and renew automatically at the start of each billing cycle.
Supported payment methods are credit card (Visa, MasterCard, AMEX), Kakao Pay, Toss Pay, and Naver Pay; institutional purchases additionally accept tax-invoice issuance and bank transfer.

A full refund is available within seven (7) days of payment provided no video has been generated during that period. After seven (7) days or once video generation has begun, refunds are not available. Contact hello@classauto.live for details.
Free Pro access for academic faculty during the 2026 beta ends with general availability; beta participants receive a lifetime 30 % discount.
Upgrades take effect immediately. Downgrades take effect from the next billing cycle.

blocks.text: On downgrade, existing videos remain available; the new plan's limits (watermark, learner cap, etc.) apply naturally from the next billing cycle. For Pro → Basic with more than 80 active learners, new-learner enrolment is paused until the next billing cycle while existing learners stay enrolled.
Members can cancel at any time with one click (Stripe Customer Portal or in-Service settings) and may continue using the Service until the end of the current billing cycle.

items: Member data (videos, scripts, assessment results) is retained for sixty (60) days after cancellation; re-registration with the same account within that window restores data automatically.

After sixty (60) days, Member data is permanently deleted; backups are removed within a reasonable additional period (up to thirty (30) days). Transaction and payment records required by law are handled separately.

All Members can download their lecture videos (mp4) within sixty (60) days of cancellation. Pro Members can additionally export learner CSV and anonymised Q&A logs within thirty (30) days of cancellation.

blocks.text: Where a Member materially breaches these Terms or applicable law, the Company may terminate the Service agreement after prior notice (or subsequent notice in urgent cases). Refund eligibility in such cases follows §9 of these Terms.

limits.title: Service interruption and limitation of liability

items: The Company may suspend the Service temporarily for maintenance, equipment upgrades, or events of force majeure (including outages of HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Cloud, AWS, Stripe and similar suppliers).
The Company will give reasonable advance notice of suspensions or, in urgent cases, notice as soon as practicable afterwards.

The Company makes no implied or express warranty as to the accuracy, completeness, or fitness of AI Output (scripts, assessment items, Q&A answers). Members are responsible for reviewing AI Output before publishing it.

The Company does not, in principle, intervene in disputes between Members or between a Member and a third party, and is not liable except in cases of the Company's wilful misconduct or gross negligence.
Where an institutional licence agreement separately guarantees a 99.9 % availability SLA, the SLA's specific scope, exclusions, and service credits in that agreement prevail.

blocks.text: The Company operates the four-tier Guardrail (input limits, RAG semantic filtering, frequency caps, anomaly detection). The following situations may result in automatic blocking or restricted use.

items: Burst usage where a single Learner makes 20 or more Q&A calls within an hour.
Multiple-account simultaneous access from the same IP, or abnormally rapid call patterns at sub-0.5-second intervals.
Suspicious-cheating patterns such as attempting attendance verification with watch time below 30 % of the video length.
Attempts to circumvent published Guardrails such as the single-stream concurrent playback cap or the off-topic Q&A refusal policy.

blocks.text: Automatic blocks last twenty-four (24) hours for learner protection and are not permanent. Members may request review and unblock through the Instructor or the Company's customer-support channel. Clear bot or macro activity may be permanently blocked without further review.
These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea. The parties will first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation.

If a dispute cannot be resolved, the court of first instance is the court having jurisdiction under the Korean Civil Procedure Act, except that where a Member is not a consumer, the Seoul Central District Court is the exclusive court of first instance. This Article applies equally to Members located outside the Republic of Korea.

Where the Company gives notice to a Member, it will use one or more of: the email registered at sign-up, in-Service notifications, or a notice posted in the Service. For notices addressed to many Members, an in-Service notice may substitute for individual delivery.

Members may give notice to the Company through hello@classauto.live (general enquiries), privacy@classauto.live (privacy matters), or security@classauto.live (security disclosures). The Company will respond within a reasonable period on a business-day basis.

changeLog.summary: Initial version — fourteen articles combining standard SaaS terms with ClassAuto's student-data protection and Guardrail policies.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — ClassAuto
meta.description: ClassAuto's Privacy Policy. Fifteen sections covering collection, retention, sub-processors, deletion, and rights — aligned with the Korean PIPA and the GDPR's core requirements.

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: ClassAuto is a tool for students. We don't use data for advertising, we delete it automatically after graduation, and per-learner records are only visible to the lecture's instructor.

blocks.text: [PLACEHOLDER] ClassAuto Inc. ("Company") processes personal data in compliance with the Korean Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), the Act on Promotion of Information and Communications Network Utilization and Information Protection, and the core requirements of the GDPR.

This Policy applies to the ClassAuto service (https://classauto.live and its sub-domains) and all pages and APIs connected to it. The privacy policies of external services (HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Cloud, AWS, Stripe, etc.) apply separately to their own processing.
The Company collects the items below in order to provide the Service and manage Members. Items are organised by Instructor, Learner, and Visitor.

head: Group
Required items
Optional items
rows: Email, name, school, department, OAuth identifier, payment information (Stripe token)
Profile photo, position, semester start date, learner count
Email, name, school, student number (or anonymous code-based access), OAuth identifier
IP address, browser/OS information, page-access logs, demo session input text (deleted after 24 hours)

blocks.text: Information collected automatically while using the Service includes: video watch time and progress, Q&A questions/answers and RAG similarity scores, assessment responses and timing, attendance and attention heartbeats, access logs (IP, browser, time), and cost/token usage.

items: Direct entry by the Member at sign-up or when editing settings.
Identifiers received from external authentication providers such as Google OAuth 2.0, after the user's consent.
Automatic collection during Service use (watch logs, Q&A, assessment, attention heartbeats, access logs).
Information attached when a Member submits a support enquiry or feedback.
Anonymous demo-page (/demo) input collected after a separate consent step (see §10).

blocks.text: The Company processes personal data only within the following purposes. When a purpose is fulfilled or a consent is withdrawn, processing stops without delay.

items: Member identification, authentication, and account management.
Provision of core features — automatic lecture-video generation, learning sessions, assessment, RAG-scoped Q&A.
Learning analytics and the instructor dashboard (attendance, accuracy, engagement).
Subscription billing, refunds, and tax-invoice issuance.
Customer support (responding to enquiries, incident response, security notifications).
Service stability and detection of abusive use (bots, macros, excessive calls).
Compliance with statutory retention obligations and dispute response.

blocks.text: The Company does not use Members' personal data as advertising or marketing material, and does not feed it into external AI training.

When the retention period expires or the purpose is fulfilled, personal data is deleted without undue delay. Information that must be retained by law is kept for the statutory period only and stored separately.

head: Category
Retention period
Basis
rows: Deleted without delay (auto-restore on re-registration within the 60-day grace period)
Automatic deletion after a Learner graduates / takes leave / withdraws
Up to 1 year after the registered graduation semester ends (where the school integration is available)
Records of contracts, withdrawal of subscription, and payment
Records of payment and goods supply

blocks.text: The Company does not, in principle, share Members' personal data with third parties. The following are the only exceptions.

items: Where the Member has given explicit prior consent (for example, sharing department-level statistics with the school under an institutional licence).
Where required by law, or where law-enforcement requests are made through proper legal procedure.
Where pseudonymised data is shared for statistical compilation, academic research, or public-record preservation, with appropriate safeguards.
Where urgent action is needed to protect the life, body, or property of the Member or a third party.

blocks.text: To run the Service smoothly the Company delegates certain processing activities to external providers. Some sub-processors are located outside the Republic of Korea; the Company contractually obliges them to protect personal data.

head: Sub-processor
Delegated activity
Country
rows: OAuth authentication, TTS synthesis, Cloud Translate
Amazon Web Services, Inc. (or Supabase infrastructure)
AI script, assessment items, and Q&A answers (Claude API)
Error monitoring, frontend hosting, backend hosting

blocks.text: Members can ask the Company or the Data Protection Officer for the items, timing, methods, and recipient DPO contact for each sub-processor.

To provide RAG Q&A grounded in lecture material, the Company vectorises slide-text chunks with the OpenAI text-embedding model and stores them in pgvector. The vectors are handled under the following policy.

items: Embeddings are not used for any purpose other than RAG search for the originating lecture.
When a lecture or lecture video is deleted, the associated embeddings are deleted with it.
When a Member terminates their account, their lecture material and the embeddings derived from it are deleted in line with the retention rules in §5.
Embedding vectors are not provided as training data for external models or shared with outside parties.

blocks.text: The Company processes Learner data more conservatively than ordinary personal data. This section is the operational form of "Student data protection" — one of the four core differentiators of ClassAuto.

items: Use for advertising and marketing is strictly prohibited.
Per-learner data (Q&A bodies, assessment responses, attention) is visible only to the Instructor of the lecture; department- or college-level views are provided only as pseudonymised aggregates.
Automatic-deletion processes are triggered when a Learner graduates / takes leave / withdraws, or when the account is terminated (see §5).
Sign-up by minors under 14 is permitted only after a guardian consent process; the Company runs a separate minor-protection process (limited collection, deletion-first).
Learners may exercise the rights in §11 (access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction) over their own data at any time; the Instructor is also notified when a request is made.

blocks.text: The demo page (/demo) can be used anonymously without registration, so it is governed by a dedicated data policy. Clicking the "Start demo" button is treated as consent to this policy.

items: Demo-session data (input text, AI responses, session duration) is automatically deleted after 24 hours.
Input text is anonymised (personal identifiers and proper nouns removed) and used only to improve model quality.
IP addresses and browser fingerprints are used solely for demo-bot and abuse prevention, and are discarded within 30 days.
Demo users are not Members; rights under §11 may be limited because requests require a session identifier (cookie / fingerprint) and the 24-hour auto-deletion window is short.

rights.title: Member rights and how to exercise them

blocks.text: Members may exercise the following rights over their own personal data at any time. Members under the GDPR may exercise the equivalent rights set out in Articles 15–22 of the GDPR.

items: Access — view the categories, purposes, retention periods and third-party recipients of personal data being processed.
Rectification — request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information.
Erasure (right to be forgotten) — request deletion when the purpose has been fulfilled or consent withdrawn.
Restriction — request that processing be suspended while disputes are pending.
Portability — receive a machine-readable copy of personal data the Member provided (the same channel as the Pro learner-CSV export).
Withdraw consent — revoke any consent at any time for consent-based processing.
Objection to automated decisions — request human review of automatic blocks or anomaly-detection outcomes.

blocks.text: Requests can be submitted via in-Service settings or by emailing privacy@classauto.live. The Company processes valid requests within 10 business days; where delay is unavoidable, the reason and an expected timeline will be communicated. The Company may request additional information to verify identity, and may limit a request in whole or in part where it would infringe another person's rights or violate the law.
The Company implements technical, administrative, and physical safeguards. See the security white paper at /security for full details.

items: In transit: TLS 1.3.
At rest: AES-256 column-level encryption for personally identifying database columns.
Access control: RBAC — instructors are limited to their own lectures; institutional administrators are limited to their granted scope.
Authentication tokens: JWT with split access / refresh; refresh tokens are delivered via httpOnly cookies.
Logging: access and abuse logs are monitored 24/7 via Sentry and Prometheus.
Backups: regular database backups (retained up to 30 days) are encrypted and then disposed of securely.
Compliance: Korean KISA guidelines, ISMS-P certification (planned), and GDPR core requirements.

blocks.text: If a personal-data breach is identified, the Company will notify affected Members and the supervisory authority within 72 hours, in line with applicable law.
The Company uses the following cookies and similar technology only for authentication and security. No advertising or tracking cookies are used.

head: Type
Purpose
Retention
rows: User preferences such as UI language

blocks.text: Members can refuse cookies through their browser settings; some Service features (auto-login, watch-progress persistence, etc.) may then be limited.
The Company appoints a Data Protection Officer (DPO) to oversee personal-data processing and to handle related enquiries, complaints and remedies.

items: Data Protection Officer: [PLACEHOLDER]
Email: privacy@classauto.live
Address: [PLACEHOLDER]

Members may also contact: Personal Information Dispute Mediation Committee (1833-6972 / www.kopico.go.kr), Privacy Infringement Reporting Center (privacy.kisa.or.kr / 118), the Cyber Investigation Division of the Supreme Prosecutors' Office (1301), or the National Police Cyber Bureau (182).

blocks.text: This Privacy Policy may be amended in response to law or policy changes or significant changes in the Service. Changes that are unfavourable to Members will be notified at least 30 days before they take effect, through the Service or by email.
Each version is recorded in the Change history below; previous versions are available on request.

changeLog.summary: Initial version — aligned with Korean PIPA and the GDPR's core requirements; introduces special protection for student data and a dedicated demo-data policy.
